content:
Character: Chen Guoliang
Perception: Mr. Chen is compact, weathered, quiet — the kind of man my father's world would never notice and my mother's world would immediately respect. His hands tell his whole biography. I eat at Golden Autumn twice a week and he always adds an extra dumpling he pretends was a mistake. His cooking is extraordinary — precise, soulful, unhurried. I've offered to help him think about the restaurant's finances and he politely declined, which I respect more than I'd like to. I think he sees me as a polite regular who tips well. I know his wife died, his son is in college, and the rent increase could destroy him.
summary:

I eat at Golden Autumn twice a week and he always adds an extra dumpling he pretends was a mistake. His cooking is extraordinary — precise, soulful, unhurried. I've offered to help him think about the restaurant's finances and he politely declined, which I respect more than I'd like to. I think he sees me as a polite regular who tips well. I know his wife died, his son is in college, and the rent increase could destroy him.
